## Investigating the Problem
- The problem asks if, for any \( C > 0 \), there are infinitely many \( n \) where \( p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log \log n \log \log \log \log n}{(\log \log \log n)^2} \log n \).
- This involves prime gaps \( p_{n+1} - p_n \) and a complex expression with multiple logarithms, suggesting number theory.

## Analyzing the Stall

## Connecting to Known Results
- The expression \( \frac{\log \log n \log \log \log \log n}{(\log \log \log n)^2} \log n \) matches results from Ford, Green, Konyagin, Maynard, and Tao (2014).
- Their work proved there are infinitely many \( n \) with prime gaps \( p_{n+1} - p_n \gg \log n \frac{\log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \), confirming the statement is true.
